title: Sophie Turner Gets Hurt, Tomb Raider Show Stops Filming
summary: Filming for the new Tomb Raider TV show has stopped because actress Sophie Turner got hurt. She plays Lara Croft, a famous character from a video game. The show is being made for Prime Video. Bosses said Sophie had a small injury, so they paused filming to let her get better. They hope to start again soon. Sophie is known from Game of Thrones and X-Men movies. The show also has other actors like Sigourney Weaver. Lara Croft is a popular hero in games, and this show is based on those games. The injury is not serious, but they are being careful.

background_read: This article is about a TV show based on the Tomb Raider video games. Tomb Raider started in 1996 and features Lara Croft, a brave archaeologist who goes on adventures. Sophie Turner is an actress who became famous for playing Sansa Stark in Game of Thrones. Prime Video is a streaming service where you can watch shows and movies. Injuries can happen during filming, especially in action scenes, so sometimes production stops to keep everyone safe. This helps actors recover and ensures the show is made well.
Article_Structure: WHO: Sophie Turner, an actress, and the Tomb Raider TV show team
WHAT: Filming paused because Sophie got a minor injury
WHERE: The show is being filmed for Prime Video
WHY: To let Sophie recover and keep production safe

perspectives.description: The show paused filming to prioritize Sophie Turner's health and recovery.
